defs: The line which marks the outer limits of an object or figure; the exterior line or edge; contour.
In art: A line drawn by pencil, pen, graver, or the like, by which the boundary of a figure is indicated.
A sketch composed of such lines; the delineation of a figure without shading.
quotes: Painters, by their outlines, colors, lights, and shadows, represent the same in their pictures.

defs: Fig.: A sketch of any scheme; a preliminary or general indication of a plan, system, discourse, course of thought, etc.; as, the outline of a speech.
quotes: But that larger grief . . . Is given in outline and no more.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Outlined (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Outlining.]

defs: Fig.: To sketch out or indicate as by an outline; to create a general framework of (a plan, system, discourse, course of thought), for which the details need to be added; as, to outline an argument or a campaign.
